title: The operating system for your schools
description: Title slide. Frames PulseEDU as one connected platform for students, teachers, families, and administrators, demonstrated at D.S. Parrott Middle School.
speakerNotes: Open with the promise: today is not a feature tour, it is one school day shown end to end across every audience. Everything you see runs on real data from D.S. Parrott Middle.

title: The demonstration flow
description: Agenda. The eight connected experiences in order: Student, Teacher, Student Support, MTSS, AST & Comp Time, Family Communication, Leadership Intelligence, School Operations.
speakerNotes: Walk the eight stops quickly. Emphasize that they connect — the same scan that opens a hall pass also takes attendance and feeds recognition.

title: Student Experience — Hall Pass
description: Hall pass and door kiosk. ID scan, destination, period-aware waiting line at capacity, teacher Companion Queue with Go Now, live timer and return check-in.
speakerNotes: Demo live in Amy Brown's room: scan a badge, pick a destination, show the waiting line at capacity, then push a student through with Go Now and show the return check-in.

title: Student Experience — Attendance, PBIS & Tour
description: The same scan marks attendance and posts PBIS points automatically; the public bilingual school tour page is how families first meet the school.
speakerNotes: Stress automation: one scan, three outcomes — pass, attendance, recognition. Then open the public tour page to show the front door for prospective families.

title: Student Experience — Points Store & Wallet
description: The PBIS economy: points earned on a scan become spendable rewards. Two catalogs — a per-teacher Classroom Store and a school-wide School Store — plus a per-student points wallet, family redemption from the Parent Portal, a Core Team fulfillment queue, and balance migration from a prior platform.
speakerNotes: Close the recognition loop: points are not just a number, they buy something. Show the School Store and a student wallet, then note families can redeem from the portal and Core Team fulfills from one queue.

title: Student Experience — Students Sign In
description: Student self-service. Students sign in with ClassLink single sign-on to see their own HeartBEAT — points, recognitions, and goals — and redeem rewards themselves from their own balance.
speakerNotes: Sign in as a student with ClassLink. Show their own HeartBEAT and let them redeem a reward from their balance — no teacher middle-step.

title: Teacher Experience — Roster
description: The Teacher Roster: FAST benchmark data, ESE/504/ELL and safety flags, learning-gain green-checks, one shared source of truth for teacher and Core Team.
speakerNotes: Open Amy Brown's Grade 7 Math roster. Point at the benchmark color-coding and the flags — this replaces a hand-kept spreadsheet.

title: Teacher Experience — Small Groups & Accommodations
description: Form small groups from shared skill gaps and log support over time; required accommodations surface at the point of instruction.
speakerNotes: Show grouping students by a shared weak standard and logging a session. Then open Pamela Martin's accommodations for about five students in ELA.

title: Student Support — HeartBEAT
description: The Invisible Student. Key message: every student should be known by someone. HeartBEAT finds students with no meaningful adult connection before the year slips by.
speakerNotes: Slow down — this is the emotional center. Say the line out loud. Then open Noah Xu to show the invisible-student flag and the connection history that explains why.

title: Student Support — Safety Plans
description: Per-student behavioral and physical safety plans built from a reusable item library, with role-based access (Guidance Counselor and Core Team edit, all staff view) and a full audit log of every change.
speakerNotes: Open a student's safety plan. Show the library-built checklist, who can edit versus view, and the audit log. The point: the adult in the room already knows the plan.

title: Student Support — Eligibility Hub
description: Attendance-based participation eligibility for athletics, clubs, and activities. Absences and tardies set each student's status; coaches see a read-only roster of their own activity; a weekly digest flags at-risk students; athletic directors, Core Team, and the front office manage it.
speakerNotes: Open the Eligibility Hub. Show how absences and tardies drive who can play this week, the coach's read-only roster, and the weekly digest that flags at-risk students before game day.

title: MTSS — Tiered Interventions
description: Tier 2 and Tier 3 intervention plans with about two months of authentic data: goals, weekly monitoring, multiple staff, and a visible progression of supports.
speakerNotes: Show the timeline, not a single entry — multiple weeks, multiple staff. Open the Tier 3 academic plans for Alina Maddox, Amelia Abbott, and Sienna Osborne.

title: AST & Comp Time
description: Additional Service Time and Comp Time on one auditable five-step path: submit, approve, complete, verify, bank — for both AST and EST Comp Time.
speakerNotes: Walk the five steps as Demo Admin approving time, and show EST Comp Time banking for Teresa Holloway. This is the payroll-defensible audit trail.

title: Family Communication — PulseDNA AI
description: The wow moment. From a rough idea, PulseDNA generates a family message, a social post, and a teleprompter script in the school's saved voice.
speakerNotes: Type the rough idea live: 'Volleyball team won tonight. Great crowd. Proud of our kids.' Generate all three outputs. Say the line: AI should learn the school's voice, not replace it.

title: Family Communication — Family HeartBEAT
description: The Parent Portal as the family's direct window into daily campus life: hall passes and attendance, tardies and lost instructional time, FAST scores with next steps, interventions and accommodations, and acknowledged school messages.
speakerNotes: If PulseDNA is the school's voice going out, this is the family's window coming in. Open the Parent Portal as a parent and walk one child's HeartBEAT — daily activity, lost-time honesty, FAST and next steps, the supports in place — then show a message acknowledged in one tap. Note sibling switching and PDF export.

title: Leadership Intelligence — Insights
description: The Insights Hub: Algebra performance, learning trajectories, school-wide trends, and actionable per-student metrics — days absent and approximate percent, points to the next FAST sub-level, and points to proficiency — that drill down to individual students.
speakerNotes: Say the line: data should guide what we do next. Open Algebra readiness, drill from a chart into a single student profile, and point at the three per-student numbers a teacher can act on.

title: Leadership Intelligence — FAST Coverage
description: The FAST Coverage Report: standards analyzed, percent of curriculum taught, and instructional gaps surfaced early enough to act on.
speakerNotes: Frame the difference between scores and coverage. Show the gaps by standard for Grade 7 — this tells leaders what was actually taught.

title: Leadership Intelligence — School Grade Projection
description: The School Grade Estimated Calculator: an estimated Florida middle-school grade per progress-monitoring window across nine components (each out of 100). FAST achievement and learning-gains components compute from live data; run it at PM1, PM2, and PM3 to see the trajectory before the official grade.
speakerNotes: Show the projected school grade at PM1, PM2, and PM3. Note that FAST components compute automatically and learning gains are projected early, then strict PM3-to-PM3 at the end.

title: School Operations — Event Ticketing
description: Free-ticket events end to end: create with quotas, email QR tickets per student, no-login volunteer scanning, and atomic first-scan-wins duplicate blocking.
speakerNotes: Create an event live, then scan the same ticket twice to show the duplicate block. No cash box, no clipboard.

title: School Operations — Digital Signage
description: Per-school digital signage for announcements, celebrations, and events, with live PBIS House Cup standings and one remote driving every TV.
speakerNotes: Show the live House Cup signage and the remote control driving multiple displays. This is school culture made visible.

title: School Operations — Parent Pick-Up
description: The Parent Pick-Up module: a curb keypad parents enter a number into, a separate walker-gate release, office-authorized QR pickup tags printed to PDF, a classroom signage tile, and a 'still on campus' reconciliation tile — with a reason-required front-office manual override.
speakerNotes: Show the curb keypad and the classroom tile updating, the walker gate, and the 'still on campus' reconciliation tile. Note QR tags are office-authorized and overrides require a reason.

title: The operating system thesis
description: Closing. PulseEDU is the operating system that connects students, teachers, families, and administrators into a single, actionable platform.
speakerNotes: Land the thesis verbatim. Tie back to the eight stops: one platform, every audience, one school day.
